page.title: How to Hire a Chief People Officer (CPO) in the UK
page.intro:
The Chief People Officer has evolved from a traditional HR function into a strategic executive role focused on organisational capability, culture, talent strategy, and workforce transformation. In 2026, the CPO is expected to be a genuine business partner — not an administrative function head. Finding someone with both strategic credibility and operational HR depth is the central challenge.

The best CPOs combine the ability to operate at board level — advising on culture, succession, and organisational design — with the operational grip to run a complex HR function across multiple jurisdictions and employment models. This combination is rare, and the market for strong CPO candidates is competitive.

The CPO role has also been reshaped by the rise of AI-driven HR tools, flexible working models, and increasing regulatory complexity around employment law. Organisations seeking a CPO in 2026 need someone who can lead through technological change, not just manage it.
considerations: Define whether you need a strategic people leader or an operational HR Director — these profiles are different and the distinction matters in the brief.
Clarify the transformation agenda — is the organisation going through a significant culture change, a restructuring, a rapid scaling phase, or a stabilisation period?
Understand the current HR team capability honestly — the CPO will inherit the team and must assess whether to develop it or change it.
Establish the CPO's relationship with the CEO and board — a CPO who does not have direct board access cannot deliver the strategic value the role requires.
Be clear on budget and authority for change — a CPO without the ability to invest in technology, talent development, or team capability is structurally constrained.
page.salaryRange: £95,000–£220,000 depending on organisation size and complexity
page.timeline: 10–14 weeks from briefing to accepted offer
page.whySearch: The best CPOs combine strategic thinking with practical HR delivery and genuine leadership presence. They are in high demand, particularly in PE-backed and transformation contexts, and rarely appear on the open market. A headhunter with a strong CPO network can identify candidates who are discreetly open to the right opportunity and approach them before the market hears about the role.
